text: 6 Minute English from bbclearningenglish .com
zh: 来自bbclearningenglish.com的六分钟英语

text: Hello and welcome to 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English.
zh: 大家好,欢迎收听BBC英语学习栏目《六分钟英语》。

text: I'm Pippa. And I'm Beth. Have you ever been scared of speaking in English?
zh: 我是Pippa。我是Beth。你有没有害怕过说英语?

text: In this episode, we're talking about why speaking in a foreign language can be so stressful.
zh: 在这一期节目中,我们要聊聊为什么说外语会让人压力这么大。

text: So, Pippa, do you ever get nervous speaking in a different language?
zh: Pippa,你说外语时会紧张吗?

text: Yes, I learnt French when I was younger
zh: 是的,我小时候学过法语,

text: and I was actually quite good at it, I think, but I was really nervous when I was speaking French.
zh: 我觉得自己其实学得还不错,但说法语的时候还是特别紧张。

text: How about you? Yeah, I've always felt like that with Spanish.
zh: 你呢?是啊,我说西班牙语时一直都是这种感觉。

text: I am scared to speak because I don't want to make mistakes, even though I know it's OK to make mistakes.
zh: 我害怕开口说话,是因为不想犯错,尽管我知道犯错是很正常的。

text: Well, in this episode of 6 Minute English, we'll be hearing about how to feel less less nervous when you speak English.
zh: 好,在这期《六分钟英语》节目中,我们将聊聊如何在说英语时减少紧张感。

text: And as usual, we'll be learning some useful new words and phrases.
zh: 像往常一样,我们也会学习一些实用的新单词和短语。

text: You can practice these with the quiz and worksheet on our website bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 你可以通过我们网站bbclearningenglish.com上的测验和练习题来巩固这些内容。

text: But first, I have a question for you, Pippa. So, English has around one million words,
zh: 不过首先,我有个问题要问你,Pippa。英语大约有一百万个单词,

text: but on average, how many words does a typical English speaker use in daily conversation?
zh: 但平均来说,一个典型的英语母语者在日常对话中会用到多少个单词呢?

text: Is it A, between 2 ,000 and 3 ,000,
B, between 20 ,000 and 30 ,000, or C, between 200 ,000 and 300 ,000?
I'm going to say A, 2 ,000 to 3 ,000.
OK, well, we will find out later in the programme.
zh: 好的,我们稍后在节目中揭晓答案。

text: Now, Pippa, you said you found it stressful speaking in French, even though you are quite good at it.
zh: Pippa,你说你说法语时觉得压力很大,尽管你其实说得很好。

text: Now someone else who has had this experience is Hanan Razak.
zh: 还有一个人也有过类似的经历,她就是Hanan Razak。

text: Hanan is a journalist at BBC Arabic and also host of the podcast Beating Speaking Anxiety.
zh: Hanan是BBC阿拉伯语频道的记者,也是播客《Beating Speaking Anxiety》的主持人。

text: Here she is telling BBC Learning English presenter Georgie about speaking English.
zh: 下面是她向BBC英语学习栏目主持人Georgie讲述自己说英语的经历。

text: My English was actually pretty good, but having conversations with people, I found it really difficult.
zh: 我的英语其实说得挺好,但真要和别人交谈时,我发现特别难。

text: So when I first joined the BBC, the Learning English team made an assessment of my English level, which they used to do for all new joiners to
zh: 我刚加入BBC的时候,英语学习团队对我的英语水平做了评估,他们通常会对所有新员工都这样做,

text: see if they need any help or courses. My results were pretty good and I was fluent
zh: 看看他们是否需要帮助或课程。我的结果相当不错,英语也算流利,

text: but on that very same day, leaving the building and going to get some coffee, I couldn't really understand what the barista was saying
zh: 但就在那天,我走出大楼去买咖啡时,却几乎听不懂咖啡师在说什么,

text: and I felt pretty nervous to order coffee and was trying to stress every single word
zh: 我点咖啡时特别紧张,努力把每个单词都咬得很清楚,

text: hoping that my grammar is correct and I'm pronouncing the words right. Yeah, I'm sure that's a situation lots of people can relate to.
zh: 生怕自己语法出错,发音不准。是啊,我相信很多人都能感同身受。

text: Hanan's results on her English assessment were pretty good, but she was still pretty nervous to speak in English,
zh: Hanan的英语评估结果相当不错,但她说英语时依然很紧张,

text: for example when ordering a coffee in English. The modifier pretty means quite or a bit.
zh: 比如用英语点咖啡的时候。这里的修饰词pretty意思是“相当”或“有点”。

text: Georgie says that Hanan's experience is something that lots of people can relate to.
zh: Georgie说Hanan的这种经历是很多人都能产生共鸣的。

text: If you relate to a situation, you know what it feels like, usually because a similar thing has happened to you.
zh: 如果你对某个情境有共鸣,说明你了解那种感觉,通常是因为你也经历过类似的事情。

text: So lots of people feel nervous speaking another language,
zh: 所以很多人在说另一种语言时都会感到紧张,

text: even if they're good at it, like Anan. We can call this speaking anxiety.
zh: 即使他们其实说得很好,就像Hanan一样。我们可以把这种现象称为“说话焦虑”。

text: means an uncomfortable feeling or worry about something that is happening or could happen in the future.
zh: 它指的是对正在发生或可能发生的事情感到不安或担忧的一种情绪。

text: So why do so many people have anxiety about speaking a foreign language?
zh: 那为什么这么多人会对说外语感到焦虑呢?

text: Experts think it's connected to the beliefs we have about
zh: 专家认为这与我们对自身的看法有关。

text: ourselves. Han Luo, professor of Chinese at Lafayette College in the United States,
zh: 美国拉法耶特学院中文教授罗涵(音译)

text: explains more to BBC Learning English presenter Georgie.
zh: 向BBC英语学习栏目主持人Georgie做了进一步解释。

text: You know, usually the beliefs that cause anxiety, especially severe anxiety, are, we call it irrational beliefs,
zh: 通常导致焦虑,尤其是严重焦虑的信念,我们称之为“非理性信念”,

text: and also like some low self -perceptions, fear of negative evaluation,
zh: 还有一些低自我认知、对负面评价的恐惧,

text: all those learner internal factors.
zh: 这些都是学习者的内在因素。

text: And Han says that learners worry about mistakes because they're scared of negative evaluation.
zh: Han说学习者之所以担心犯错,是因为害怕被负面评价。

text: In other words, that people will judge them for their mistakes and think badly of them.
zh: 换句话说,就是害怕别人因为他们的错误而评判他们,对他们产生不好的看法。

text: Han argues that irrational beliefs can make us anxious.
zh: Han认为非理性信念会让我们感到焦虑。

text: Irrational beliefs are beliefs that aren't based on things that are true. They're not logical.
zh: 非理性信念是指那些并非基于事实、不合逻辑的信念。

text: Hans says some people who have speaking anxiety have low self -perceptions.
zh: Han说一些有说话焦虑的人自我认知较低。

text: Your self -perception is what you think about yourself.
zh: 你的自我认知就是你对自己的看法。

text: Self -perception and irrational beliefs are both internal factors.
zh: 自我认知和非理性信念都属于内在因素。

text: They come from inside your head, not from the people around you.
zh: 它们来自你的内心,而不是来自你周围的人。

text: So for example, when you speak your first language, you might feel confident and intelligent.
zh: 比如说,当你说母语时,你可能会感到自信又聪明。

text: But when you speak in a new language, you make mistakes and feel less confident.
zh: 但当你说一门新语言时,你会犯错,也会觉得不那么自信。

text: You can't show people what you're really like and this is frustrating.
zh: 你没法向别人展示真实的自己,这会让人感到很沮丧。

text: But as we tell people all the time at BBC Learning English, mistakes are normal.
zh: 但正如我们在BBC英语学习栏目一直强调的那样,犯错是很正常的。

text: You have to try to speak even though you make mistakes. And mistakes can be a good thing. They can help us learn.
zh: 即使会犯错,你也要努力开口去说。而且犯错其实是件好事,它能帮助我们学习。

text: And if you want more tips to improve your speaking, listen to the Beating Speaking Anxiety podcast.
zh: 如果你想获得更多提升口语的技巧,可以收听播客《Beating Speaking Anxiety》。

text: There's a link in the notes below this episode. But Beth, I think it's time for the answer to your quiz question.
zh: 本期节目说明里附有链接。不过Beth,我想是时候公布你那道测验题的答案了。

text: Yes, I asked you how many words does the average English speaker use in daily communication?
zh: 好的,我之前问的是,普通英语母语者在日常交流中平均会用到多少个单词?

text: The answer is 20 ,000 to 30 ,000.
That's quite a lot. Yeah, that's loads. OK, let's recap the vocabulary we've learnt.
zh: 那还真不少。是啊,相当多。好,我们来回顾一下今天学到的词汇。

text: Pretty can be used as a modifier to mean quite or a bit.
zh: Pretty可以作为修饰词,意思是“相当”或“有点”。

text: If you relate to a person or situation, you understand them. usually because you've experienced something similar.
zh: 如果你对某个人或某种情境有共鸣(relate to),说明你理解他们,通常是因为你有过类似的经历。

text: Anxiety is feeling uncomfortable or worried about something that is happening or could happen in the future.
zh: Anxiety指的是对正在发生或可能发生的事情感到不安或担忧。

text: Irrational beliefs are beliefs about something that is not true. They're not logical.
zh: 非理性信念(Irrational beliefs)是指关于某事的、并不真实的信念,它们不合逻辑。

text: Self -perception is the way you see yourself. And internal means inside the body or mind.
zh: 自我认知(Self-perception)是你看待自己的方式。而internal指的是身体或内心的“内在”。

text: Once again, our six minutes are up. But if you'd like to find out more about speaking anxiety,
zh: 我们的六分钟又到时间了。不过如果你想更多了解说话焦虑,

text: then head over to our website bbclearningenglish .com to find our Beating Speaking Anxiety series.
zh: 欢迎访问我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com,查看我们的《Beating Speaking Anxiety》系列节目。

text: There are podcasts about the things we find scary when speaking a different language,
zh: 里面有关于我们在说外语时感到害怕的各种事情的播客,

text: and videos with tips to help you feel more confident with speaking. Thanks for joining us and Goodbye!
zh: 还有一些视频,教你一些技巧,帮助你更自信地开口说话。感谢大家收听,再见!
